wines.name: Silberlack Riesling GG
wines.oak_regime: Aged in traditional Stueckfass oak casks of about 1,200 litres

taste.summary: Dry Grosses Gewachs from the Johannisberg quartzite slope: ripe yellow stone fruit, smoky slate and concentrated salty length under a silver-foiled capsule.

pairings.why: Wine's slate-driven acidity and citrus mirror brown-butter and lemon on the freshwater fish.
The cuvee's density and yellow fruit echo the morel cream while racy acid refreshes the palate.
Salty slate minerality plays against the nutty Bavarian alpine cheese.

history.summary: Silberlack is Schloss Johannisberg's silver-foiled dry flagship from the Johannisberg quartzite hillside; the Erstes Gewaechs / GG tier launched in the 1990s and is bottled only in top vintages.

wines.description: The silver-foil-capsuled dry Grosses Gewachs from the Johannisberg quartzite slope, the flagship dry Riesling of the 1720 monastic estate.

wines.name: Goldlack Riesling Trockenbeerenauslese
wines.oak_regime: Long fermentation and ageing in oak Stueckfass

taste.summary: Schloss Johannisberg's gold-foiled Trockenbeerenauslese from individually selected botrytised berries: liqueur-like, candied stone fruit and saffron over racy slate acidity.

pairings.why: Botrytis sweetness and racy slate acid cut the fat of seared liver without cloying.
The TBA's candied-fruit core ties together blue cheese funk and quince sweetness.
The caramelised apple in the tart finds a peer in the wine's saffron-laced raisined richness.

history.summary: Schloss Johannisberg's gold-foiled TBA descends from the 1775 monastic 'Spaetlese' courier story when delayed harvest authorisation produced the documented late-harvest sweet Riesling that gave the Spaetlese category its name; the gold capsule sits at the top of the colour-coded Pradikat hierarchy.

wines.description: The gold-foiled Schloss Johannisberg Trockenbeerenauslese, made only in vintages where botrytis selection allows, in the lineage of the 1775 Spaetlese discovery.

wines.name: Rosalack Riesling Beerenauslese

taste.summary: Schloss Johannisberg's pink-foiled Beerenauslese from selected botrytised berries: candied peach, dried apricot and honey balanced by Rheingau Riesling acidity.

pairings.why: The wine's apricot core lines up directly with the stone-fruit pastry.
Honey-sweet richness wraps salty blue cheese while acidity keeps the palate refreshed.
Caramelised sugar and vanilla find a peer in the wine's honeyed, candied stone fruit.

history.summary: The pink-foiled Beerenauslese in the Schloss Johannisberg colour ladder, made only in vintages where late-harvest botrytised berries can be hand-selected; mid-19th-century reference Pradikat wine for the estate.

wines.description: The pink-foiled Schloss Johannisberg Beerenauslese, late-harvest botrytised Riesling in the Pradikat tier below the Goldlack TBA.

wines.name: Gruenlack Riesling Spaetlese

taste.summary: Schloss Johannisberg's green-foiled fruity-sweet Spaetlese: ripe yellow apple, peach and Rheingau slate over crisp acidity and lingering residual sweetness.

pairings.why: Off-dry sweetness cools the chili while citrus and slate refresh against coconut richness.
Spaetlese sweetness flatters tangy caper-cream while acidity lifts veal meatballs.
Yellow-fruit sweetness tames paprika in the cheese spread while Riesling acidity slices the richness.

history.summary: Schloss Johannisberg records 1775 as the documented Spaetlese vintage that gave the category its name, when the courier carrying harvest permission from the Fulda Abbot was delayed and botrytised grapes were vinified separately; the green-foiled Spaetlese is the colour-coded successor to that 1775 late harvest.

wines.description: Schloss Johannisberg's green-foiled fruity-sweet Spaetlese on the Johannisberg quartzite hill, in the lineage of the 1775 monastic Spaetlese discovery.

wines.name: Gelblack Riesling Trocken

taste.summary: Schloss Johannisberg's yellow-foiled estate dry Riesling: green apple, lime and slaty minerality at a friendly weekday price band.

pairings.why: Citrus snap and slate minerality slice through brown butter on the freshwater fish.
High-acid Riesling cuts the herbal bitterness of white asparagus, a German springtime fixture.
Crisp Riesling acidity counters the oily salt-cured herring and onion garnish.

history.summary: The yellow-foiled estate-level dry Riesling in the Schloss Johannisberg colour scheme, drawn from the Johannisberg quartzite slope as a more approachable counterpart to the Silberlack GG.

wines.description: Schloss Johannisberg's entry-level dry estate Riesling in the yellow-foiled tier of the colour-coded ladder.

wines.name: Bronzelack Riesling Auslese

taste.summary: Schloss Johannisberg's bronze-foiled fruity-sweet Auslese: golden stone fruit, honey-touched botrytis and racy Rheingau acidity for long cellar life.

pairings.why: Honeyed Auslese sweetness echoes the sweet mustard while acidity refreshes the veal sausage.
Botrytised honey-sweet richness tames the blue cheese's salty funk.
The Auslese's peach core mirrors the caramelised fruit on the cake.

history.summary: Schloss Johannisberg's bronze-foiled Auslese sits between the green-foiled Spaetlese and the pink-foiled Beerenauslese in the colour-coded Pradikat hierarchy; archival labels from the 1820s record the tier.

wines.description: Schloss Johannisberg's bronze-foiled fruity-sweet Auslese, made when botrytised bunches allow.

wines.name: Steinberger Riesling GG
wines.oak_regime: Aged in traditional large oak Stueckfass casks

taste.summary: Dry Grosses Gewachs from the 1136 Cistercian Steinberg walled vineyard: deep, herbal, smoky Riesling with the structure of a true single-cru wall-enclosed site.

pairings.why: The Steinberger's depth and herbal spice ties to the river-fish flesh and Riesling reduction.
Smoky slate and yellow fruit echo dark-poultry herbs while racy Riesling acid refreshes.
Salty slate minerality plays against the nutty Bavarian hard cheese.

history.summary: The Steinberg is the walled monastery vineyard documented from 1136 when the Cistercians of Eberbach enclosed it with a 3-km stone wall; Steinberger as a single-vineyard Riesling label is on record from the 19th century and reasserted as a GG in 1971 onwards.

wines.description: The walled-vineyard dry Grosses Gewachs from the 1136 Cistercian Steinberg above Eltville, the historic monopole of the Hessian state estate.

wines.name: Steinberger Riesling Kabinett Trocken

taste.summary: Dry Steinberger Kabinett: light-bodied, racy, slate-driven Riesling at low alcohol, the everyday version of the walled-vineyard cuvee.

pairings.why: Crisp low-alcohol Kabinett slices through oily salt-cured herring with onion.
Racy lime acidity matches the herbaceous spear and lightens the buttery sauce.
Light-bodied Riesling with citrus and slate minerality refreshes rice vinegar and raw fish.

history.summary: The dry Kabinett tier of the Steinberger range, drawn from the same 1136 Cistercian walled vineyard as the GG and aimed at lower-alcohol everyday drinking.

wines.description: Dry Kabinett-tier Steinberger Riesling from the 1136 Cistercian walled vineyard at Kloster Eberbach.

wines.name: Erbacher Marcobrunn Riesling GG
wines.oak_regime: Aged in traditional Stueckfass oak casks

taste.summary: Dry Grosses Gewachs from the Marcobrunn springwater vineyard on the Erbach bank: dense, broad, mineral Riesling with weight and length.

pairings.why: The wine's broad mineral palate matches the rich freshwater fish and lemon-butter.
Density and yellow fruit echo morel cream while acidity refreshes the palate.
Salty slate minerality plays against the nutty alpine cheese.

wines.description: The Hessian state estate's dry Grosses Gewachs Riesling from the Marcobrunn springwater vineyard on the Erbach bank.

wines.name: Ruedesheimer Berg Schlossberg Riesling GG

taste.summary: Dry Grosses Gewachs from the Ruedesheim Berg quartzite slope: smoky, taut, intensely mineral Riesling with citrus drive over decades of structure.

pairings.why: Density and smoky slate echo the caramelised onion crust while Riesling acid refreshes.
The cuvee's mineral length plays against earthy mushroom and the river fish flesh.
Salty slate and citrus drive the long-aged cow-milk cheese into focus.

history.summary: The Berg Schlossberg is the historic quartzite slope behind the Ruedesheim castle ruins, documented as a top single vineyard from the medieval period; the state estate works a parcel of it as Grosses Gewachs.

wines.description: The Hessian state estate's Berg Schlossberg dry Grosses Gewachs Riesling from the Ruedesheim quartzite slope.

wines.name: Assmannshaeuser Hoellenberg Spaetburgunder GG

pairings.why: The wine's red cherry core ties directly to the fruit sauce while fine tannin frames the duck.
Smoky slate and red fruit echo the gamey flesh and forest notes.

history.summary: The Hoellenberg above Assmannshausen is the historic German Pinot Noir cradle on red phyllite slate; the state estate's Domaene Assmannshausen has worked the slope since the 19th century and bottles it as Grosses Gewachs.

wines.description: The Hessian state estate's Assmannshausen Hoellenberg dry Grosses Gewachs Spaetburgunder from the red slate of the historic German Pinot Noir slope.

wines.name: Hochheimer Domdechaney Riesling GG

wines.description: The Hessian state estate's dry Grosses Gewachs Riesling from the Hochheim Domdechaney chalky loess vineyard.

Kerosene-petrol Riesling notes and smoky slate match the smoked river fish and horseradish punch.

wines.description: The Hessian state estate's dry Grosses Gewachs Riesling from the Rauenthaler Baiken Taunus phyllite slope.
